paper_title: Experimental set-up: insolation forcing and boundary conditions

methods.description: This method involves conducting two time-slice experiments using the EC-Earth model to study pre-industrial and Mid-Holocene climate conditions. The experiments aim to understand the impact of insolation changes on monsoon dynamics and other climate processes.

steps.description: Set up the EC-Earth model at T159L62 resolution for both pre-industrial and Mid-Holocene experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: configure EC-Earth model for climate experiments

steps.description: Set greenhouse gas concentrations for the Mid-Holocene experiment to pre-industrial values slightly different from those of 1850.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust greenhouse gas concentrations for Mid-Holocene

steps.description: Run the EC-Earth model for 50 years for both PI and MH experiments, using the last 40 years for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: execute climate model simulations for 50 years

steps.description: Analyze trends in surface air temperature, sea surface temperature, surface heat flux, top-of-atmosphere net flux, and precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze climate trends from simulation data

steps.description: Calculate the globally averaged tendency term of surface air temperature, dT/dt, to ensure it is near-zero.
